diagrams.title: BFS vs DFS Exploration Order
diagrams.description: Shows how BFS explores level-by-level (A→B→C→D→E→F→G) while DFS goes deep first (A→B→E→F→C→G→D)
elements: BFS arrows showing horizontal level expansion
DFS arrows showing vertical depth-first path
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different exploration strategies and their order of node visit

diagrams.title: Hill Climbing Local Maximum Problem
diagrams.description: Illustration showing a search landscape with multiple peaks - highlights how greedy approach gets stuck at local maximum instead of global maximum
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing why Hill Climbing fails at local optima and need for variants

diagrams.title: Search Algorithm Classification
diagrams.description: Tree diagram showing classification: Uninformed (BFS, DFS, Uniform Cost) vs Informed (Hill Climbing, A*), and their properties
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where each algorithm fits in AI search taxonomy
